text.title: Early Intervention Signals and Save Offers
text.summary: Signal-to-action table for detecting at-risk users before cancellation, re-engagement email structure, and save-offer design at the cancellation flow.

list.item: no login 7 days → automated re-engagement email
no login 14 days → personal email from a named team member
feature usage <20% of baseline → "did you know?" feature tip email
negative support ticket → manager follow-up within 24h
payment failure → dunning sequence tied to specific decline code

rule.statement: Tier the response by churn reason: price objections get a discount, value objections get a CSM call, feature gaps get a roadmap link — never a flat 50% off for all segments.
rule.rationale: Reason-matched interventions preserve margin and avoid training the entire cohort to use cancellation as a discount lever.

rule.statement: Cap save-offer eligibility at once per 12 months per user and track repeat-savers; users who accept a save offer and cancel again within 90 days are negative-LTV.
rule.rationale: Unlimited save offers convert initially then crater NRR as repeat-savers extract discounts without genuine re-engagement.

list.item: time objection → account pause for 30 days
feature gap → free upgrade to next tier for 1 month
competitor move → acknowledge and offer a success-call to address the specific gap

antipattern.description: Agents inventing save offers exceeding 50% discount or extending trials beyond 30 days without hard constraints.
antipattern.reason: LLMs will generate generous offers unless constrained; unconstrained offers destroy unit economics and the agent cannot see margin data.

rule.statement: Tie dunning email copy to the specific Stripe decline code (insufficient_funds vs lost_card vs do_not_honor); generic "update card" loses recoverable involuntary churn.
rule.rationale: Different decline codes have different recovery rates and appropriate CTAs; a "lost card" decline needs a new card link, not a "try again" button.
